libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No environment variables or system properties are needed. If tests define custom org.json4s.Reader instances for 3.7.0-M16, implement both read and readEither or use Reader.from/Reader.fromPartialFunction; the reported failure is an API-change compile issue, not missing external setup.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #7182 [Automation] java run fails for org.json4s:json4s-jackson_2.13:3.7.0-M16; label=fails-java-run; body_chars=8015

risks: Existing M13 test code that implemented Reader.read only will not compile on M16 because Reader now also requires readEither.
Native-image reachability for json4s extraction/serialization still depends on exercising reflective case-class mappings and validating the generated metadata locally.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: json4s-jackson_2.13 is an in-process Scala/Jackson JSON library; its resolved POM brings json4s-core, json4s-jackson-core, Jackson databind, scala-library, json4s-scalap, and paranamer transitively, and normal documented usage is just imports plus parsing/serialization APIs. No Docker service, environment/system property, or extra Maven dependency is needed for meaningful coverage.
